HTE Analysis using BART: EOLIA application

Zhe Chen

Analysis:

  • excluding control patients who switched to ECMO
  • limited to patients enrolled with PaO2/FiO2 <80 mm Hg with FiO2 >=80% for >6 hours

Example Usage

Data Cleaning and Standardization

# Load the dataset
load("../../../data/EOLIA_04122017/eolia_form.RData")
# Clean up variable types and remove rows with missing data
dat <- eolia[, c("ID1792S8V403", "age", "sexe", "ards_etiology_regroup3",
                 "pao2_fio2_rando", "paco2_rando",
                 "vasopressors_rando", "prone_rando", "manoeuvre_rando",
                 "steroid_rando", "sepsis_class_bin", "immunocomp_adm",
                 "lactate_rando", "rrt_rando", "sofa_rando_bin", "del_intub_rando_bin",
                 "compliance_rando", "driving_rando",
                 "ecmo_centers.f", 
                 "bras_en", "statut_vital_j60", "rescue")]

names(dat)[1] <- "enrollment_criteria"
levels(dat$enrollment_criteria) <- c("PaO2/FiO2 <50 mm Hg with FiO2 >=80% > 3 hours", "PaO2/FiO2 <80 mm Hg with FiO2 >=80% for >6 hours", "pH < 7.25 with PaCO2 >=60 mm for >6 hours", "Non dispo")
dat$enrollment_criteria <- factor(dat$enrollment_criteria)
addmargins(table(dat$bras_en, dat$statut_vital_j60))
##          
##             0   1 Sum
##   ECMO     80  44 124
##   Control  68  57 125
##   Sum     148 101 249
cat("Exclusion of patients receiving rescue ECMO: \n")
## Exclusion of patients receiving rescue ECMO:
sum(dat$rescue)
## [1] 35
dat <- dat[dat$rescue == 0, ]
dat$rescue <- NULL

cat("Selection of patients enrolled with the PaO2/FiO2 <80 mm Hg with FiO2 >=80% for >6 hours criteria: \n")
## Selection of patients enrolled with the PaO2/FiO2 <80 mm Hg with FiO2 >=80% for >6 hours criteria:
dat <- dat[as.numeric(dat$enrollment_criteria) == 2, ]
dat$enrollment_criteria <- NULL

cat("Number of missing observations for each variables:\n")
## Number of missing observations for each variables:
sapply(dat, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
##                    age                   sexe ards_etiology_regroup3 
##                      0                      0                      0 
##        pao2_fio2_rando            paco2_rando     vasopressors_rando 
##                      1                      1                      0 
##            prone_rando        manoeuvre_rando          steroid_rando 
##                      0                      0                      0 
##       sepsis_class_bin         immunocomp_adm          lactate_rando 
##                      0                      1                      9 
##              rrt_rando         sofa_rando_bin    del_intub_rando_bin 
##                      0                      0                      0 
##       compliance_rando          driving_rando         ecmo_centers.f 
##                     37                     37                      0 
##                bras_en       statut_vital_j60 
##                      0                      0
cat("Suppression of compliance and driving pressure variables (too many missing values): \n")
## Suppression of compliance and driving pressure variables (too many missing values):
dat$compliance_rando <- dat$driving_rando <- NULL
dat <- dat[complete.cases(dat), ]
dat$pao2_fio2_rando <- round(dat$pao2_fio2_rando)

cat("Number of observations in the EOLIA database: ", nrow(eolia), "\n")
## Number of observations in the EOLIA database:  249
cat("Number of observations in the analysis database: ", nrow(dat), "\n")
## Number of observations in the analysis database:  151
addmargins(table(dat$bras_en, dat$statut_vital_j60))
##          
##             0   1 Sum
##   ECMO     50  36  86
##   Control  36  29  65
##   Sum      86  65 151
# Print out summaries of continuous covariates pre-standardization
cat("Mean and SD of age (pre-standardization):\n")
## Mean and SD of age (pre-standardization):
cat("Mean:", mean(dat$age, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Mean: 53.15232
cat("SD:", sd(dat$age, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## SD: 13.17055
cat("Mean and SD of PaO2/FiO2 (pre-standardization):\n")
## Mean and SD of PaO2/FiO2 (pre-standardization):
cat("Mean:", mean(dat$pao2_fio2_rando, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Mean: 67.29139
cat("SD:", sd(dat$pao2_fio2_rando, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## SD: 18.39224
cat("Mean and SD of PaCO2 (pre-standardization):\n")
## Mean and SD of PaCO2 (pre-standardization):
cat("Mean:", mean(dat$paco2_rando, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Mean: 52.59603
cat("SD:", sd(dat$paco2_rando, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## SD: 14.0291
cat("Mean and SD of Lactate (pre-standardization):\n")
## Mean and SD of Lactate (pre-standardization):
cat("Mean:", mean(dat$lactate_rando, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Mean: 3.186093
cat("SD:", sd(dat$lactate_rando, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## SD: 3.241276
# Standardize continuous covariates: age
contvar_unscaled <- dat[, c("age", "pao2_fio2_rando", "paco2_rando", "lactate_rando")]
dat$age <- (dat$age - mean(dat$age)) / sd(dat$age)
dat$pao2_fio2_rando <- (dat$pao2_fio2_rando - mean(dat$pao2_fio2_rando)) / sd(dat$pao2_fio2_rando)
dat$paco2_rando <- (dat$paco2_rando - mean(dat$paco2_rando)) / sd(dat$paco2_rando)
dat$lactate_rando <- (dat$lactate_rando - mean(dat$lactate_rando)) / sd(dat$lactate_rando)

# Treatment variable as logical
dat$bras_en <- ifelse(dat$bras_en == "ECMO", TRUE, FALSE)
# Rename covariates for easier use in decision tree plotting
colnames(dat) <- c("Age", "Sexe", "ARDS etiology", "PaO2/FiO2", 
                   "PaCO2", "Vasopressors", "Prone position", "Recruitment maneuvers",
                   "Corticosteroids", "Severe sepsis or septic shock",
                   "Immunocompromised", "Lactate", "Renal replacement therapy",
                   "SOFA score", "Interval between start on MV and randomization",
                   "Center",
                   "z", "dead60")

# Set up variables for the model
z <- dat[, "z"]  # Binary treatment variable
y <- dat[, "dead60"]  # Outcome variable (dead60)
X <- dat[, !(names(dat) %in% c("z", "dead60"))]  # Covariates

X_unscaled <- X
X_unscaled$Age <- contvar_unscaled$age
X_unscaled$`PaO2/FiO2` <- contvar_unscaled$pao2_fio2_rando
X_unscaled$PaCO2 <- contvar_unscaled$paco2_rando
X_unscaled$Lactate <- contvar_unscaled$lactate_rando

# Check the updated data structure
head(dat)
##           Age Sexe       ARDS etiology  PaO2/FiO2      PaCO2 Vasopressors
## 1  -0.6189810  Man Bacterial pneumonia -0.1789554 -0.3276066           No
## 2  -1.2263969  Man               Other -1.5925950  0.3851974          Yes
## 5  -0.9226889  Man               Other  0.1472691  0.8128798           No
## 7   0.2921429  Man               Other  1.7240210  0.6703190          Yes
## 8   0.5199239  Man               Other -0.2333261 -0.4701674          Yes
## 11  0.2921429  Man               Other  0.2016399 -0.5414478          Yes
##    Prone position Recruitment maneuvers Corticosteroids
## 1              No                    No              No
## 2              No                    No              No
## 5              No                   Yes              No
## 7              No                    No              No
## 8              No                    No              No
## 11             No                    No             Yes
##    Severe sepsis or septic shock Immunocompromised    Lactate
## 1                            Yes                No -0.5201941
## 2                            Yes                No -0.4584900
## 5                            Yes                No -0.7053063
## 7                            Yes               Yes  5.4033985
## 8                            Yes                No -0.1808216
## 11                           Yes                No -0.2425257
##    Renal replacement therapy SOFA score
## 1                        Yes  SOFA < 11
## 2                         No SOFA >= 11
## 5                        Yes  SOFA < 11
## 7                        Yes SOFA >= 11
## 8                        Yes SOFA >= 11
## 11                       Yes SOFA >= 11
##    Interval between start on MV and randomization       Center     z dead60
## 1                                         <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      0
## 2                                         <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      0
## 5                                       >= 3 days ECMO centers FALSE      0
## 7                                         <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      1
## 8                                         <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      1
## 11                                        <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      0

Description of the database :

library(gtsummary)
## 
## Attaching package: 'gtsummary'
## The following object is masked from 'package:flextable':
## 
##     continuous_summary
dat |> 
  mutate(z.f = factor(z, c(TRUE, FALSE), c("ECMO", "Control"))) |>
  tbl_summary(
    by = z.f
  )
Characteristic ECMO
N = 861		 Control
N = 651
Age 0.06 (-0.62, 0.52) 0.14 (-0.62, 0.90)
Sexe

    Man 58 (67%) 48 (74%)
    Woman 28 (33%) 17 (26%)
ARDS etiology

    Bacterial pneumonia 37 (43%) 28 (43%)
    Viral pneumonia 17 (20%) 6 (9.2%)
    Other 32 (37%) 31 (48%)
PaO2/FiO2 -0.23 (-0.61, 0.36) -0.07 (-0.45, 0.53)
PaCO2 -0.08 (-0.54, 0.39) -0.11 (-0.76, 0.46)
Vasopressors 65 (76%) 47 (72%)
Prone position 54 (63%) 39 (60%)
Recruitment maneuvers 13 (15%) 17 (26%)
Corticosteroids 32 (37%) 21 (32%)
Severe sepsis or septic shock 68 (79%) 48 (74%)
Immunocompromised 19 (22%) 18 (28%)

Renal replacement therapy 20 (23%) 23 (35%)

    SOFA < 11 42 (49%) 27 (42%)
    SOFA >= 11 44 (51%) 38 (58%)

    <3 days 59 (69%) 45 (69%)
    >= 3 days 27 (31%) 20 (31%)

z 86 (100%) 0 (0%)
dead60 36 (42%) 29 (45%)
1 Median (Q1, Q3); n (%)

Step 1: Fit a BART model to estimate CATEs

# Hyperparameter values selected via cross-validation
cvresults_mort <- c(3, 0.25, 400)  # Power, Base, Ntrees

# Fit the BART model
step1.res <- bart_fit(X, y, z, Power = cvresults_mort[1], Base = cvresults_mort[2], Ntrees = cvresults_mort[3])
## [1]  744 4148 3964   18
## [1] 2982 2379 8349 1086

Step 2: Fit a CART model to the estimated CATE values from BART

cate.res = step1.res$cate_results
cart_fit(
  point_est = cate.res$cate,
  posterior = step1.res$cate_posterior,
  X         = X_unscaled,
  metric    = "Risk Difference",
  maxdepth = 3
)

## 
## ==== RISK DIFFERENCE SUBGROUPS ====
## 
## Node: 10   n = 35 
##   Mean Risk Difference : 0 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.158 , 0.165 ]
## 
## Node: 9   n = 24 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.002 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.199 , 0.184 ]
## 
## Node: 5   n = 27 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.003 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.16 , 0.16 ]
## 
## Node: 12   n = 14 
##   Mean Risk Difference : 0.001 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.214 , 0.203 ]
## 
## Node: 4   n = 30 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.004 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.17 , 0.164 ]
## 
## Node: 13   n = 8 
##   Mean Risk Difference : 0.004 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.201 , 0.2 ]
## 
## Node: 6   n = 13 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.001 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.212 , 0.206 ]

Plot: Estimated CATEs (solid line) with 95% Credible Intervals (shaded area) for each individual

# Sort the results by CATE values for better visualization
cate.res <- cate.res[order(cate.res$cate, decreasing = TRUE), ]
cate.res$index <- 1:nrow(cate.res)  # Add an index for plotting

# Customize y-axis scales
# scales_y <- list(
#   `Mortality` = scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-0.35, 0.25, 0.1))
# )

# Set the outcome label for facet plotting
cate.res$outcome <- "Mortality"

# Generate the CATE plot
cate.plot <- ggplot(cate.res, aes(x = index, y = cate)) +
  facet_wrap(~outcome, scales = "free") +
  geom_smooth(aes(ymin = cate_lower, ymax = cate_upper), stat = "identity",
              colour = "black", fill = "grey", alpha = 0.4) +  # Add shading for CI
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 3) +  # Horizontal line at 0 effect
  xlab("Participant (sorted by effect size)") + ylab("Estimated CATE") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_fill_grey() +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(round(min(cate.res$cate_lower), 1), round(max(cate.res$cate_upper), 1), by = 0.1))
#   ggh4x::facetted_pos_scales(y = list(
#   outcome == "Mortality" ~ scale_y_continuous(breaks = round(seq(-0.3, 0.2, 0.1), 1),
#                                               limits = c(-0.33, 0.26))
# ))


# Output plot to console
cate.plot

# Optionally, save the plot as a PDF
# pdf(file = "CATE_plot.pdf")
# print(cate.plot)
# dev.off()

summary(cate.res)
##       cate              cate_lower        cate_upper           rr       
##  Min.   :-0.0081310   Min.   :-0.4295   Min.   :0.2156   Min.   :1.012  
##  1st Qu.:-0.0033186   1st Qu.:-0.3578   1st Qu.:0.2879   1st Qu.:1.064  
##  Median :-0.0016472   Median :-0.3315   Median :0.3236   Median :1.112  
##  Mean   :-0.0014004   Mean   :-0.3252   Mean   :0.3208   Mean   :1.124  
##  3rd Qu.: 0.0006512   3rd Qu.:-0.2946   3rd Qu.:0.3541   3rd Qu.:1.173  
##  Max.   : 0.0047349   Max.   :-0.2119   Max.   :0.4170   Max.   :1.288  
##     rr_lower         rr_upper     prob_treated_mean prob_control_mean
##  Min.   :0.2440   Min.   :1.453   Min.   :0.1667    Min.   :0.1660   
##  1st Qu.:0.3263   1st Qu.:2.089   1st Qu.:0.2807    1st Qu.:0.2828   
##  Median :0.3994   Median :2.502   Median :0.4318    Median :0.4331   
##  Mean   :0.4111   Mean   :2.579   Mean   :0.4266    Mean   :0.4280   
##  3rd Qu.:0.4849   3rd Qu.:3.089   3rd Qu.:0.5490    3rd Qu.:0.5495   
##  Max.   :0.6689   Max.   :4.030   Max.   :0.7682    Max.   :0.7724   
##      index            outcome   
##  Min.   :  1.0   Length   :151  
##  1st Qu.: 38.5   N.unique :  1  
##  Median : 76.0   N.blank  :  0  
##  Mean   : 76.0   Min.nchar:  9  
##  3rd Qu.:113.5   Max.nchar:  9  
##  Max.   :151.0
# CATE distribution summary for reporting
# Recompute cate_posterior from stored model objects (not returned by bart_fit in this version)
prob_treated <- exp(step1.res$bartmod1$yhat.test.collapse) /
               (1 + exp(step1.res$bartmod1$yhat.test.collapse))
prob_control <- exp(step1.res$bartmod0$yhat.test.collapse) /
               (1 + exp(step1.res$bartmod0$yhat.test.collapse))
cate_posterior <- prob_treated - prob_control

# ATE = mean of individual CATEs, with posterior uncertainty from the MCMC draws
ate_draws <- rowMeans(cate_posterior)
ate_ci    <- quantile(ate_draws, c(0.025, 0.975))

ind_cate  <- step1.res$cate_results$cate

cat("=== CATE Summary for Reporting ===\n\n")
## === CATE Summary for Reporting ===
cat("Mean ARR (ATE)        :", round(mean(ate_draws), 4), "\n")
## Mean ARR (ATE)        : -0.0014
cat("95% Credible Interval :", round(ate_ci[1], 4), "to", round(ate_ci[2], 4), "\n")
## 95% Credible Interval : -0.135 to 0.13
cat("SD (individual CATEs) :", round(sd(ind_cate), 4), "\n")
## SD (individual CATEs) : 0.0026
cat("IQR                   :", round(quantile(ind_cate, 0.25), 4),
    "to", round(quantile(ind_cate, 0.75), 4), "\n")
## IQR                   : -0.0033 to 7e-04
cat("Range                 :", round(min(ind_cate), 4),
    "to", round(max(ind_cate), 4), "\n")
## Range                 : -0.0081 to 0.0047
cat("Proportion ARR > 0    :", round(mean(ind_cate > 0), 4), "\n")
## Proportion ARR > 0    : 0.298
# Patients with positive ARR point estimate (potential harm) — specific to P/F <80 analysis
harm_idx <- ind_cate > 0
n_harm   <- sum(harm_idx)
cat("Patients with ARR > 0 (potential harm):", n_harm, "/", length(ind_cate), "\n\n")
## Patients with ARR > 0 (potential harm): 45 / 151
if (n_harm > 0) {
  harm_cate      <- ind_cate[harm_idx]
  ate_harm_draws <- rowMeans(cate_posterior[, harm_idx, drop = FALSE])
  ate_harm_ci    <- quantile(ate_harm_draws, c(0.025, 0.975))

  cat("ARR in potential harm subset:\n")
  cat("  Mean ARR              :", round(mean(harm_cate), 4), "\n")
  cat("  Range                 :", round(min(harm_cate), 4),
      "to", round(max(harm_cate), 4), "\n")
  cat("  Mean ARR (ATE, posterior) :", round(mean(ate_harm_draws), 4), "\n")
  cat("  95% Credible Interval :", round(ate_harm_ci[1], 4),
      "to", round(ate_harm_ci[2], 4), "\n")
}
## ARR in potential harm subset:
##   Mean ARR              : 0.0017 
##   Range                 : 3e-04 to 0.0047 
##   Mean ARR (ATE, posterior) : 0.0017 
##   95% Credible Interval : -0.1458 to 0.1474

Diagnostics

Next consider diagnostics for the models with the binary mortality outcome as described in Sparapani (2021). First consider the autocorrelation of the estimated response surface from BART from 10 randomly selected subjects. This may start somewhat correlated for nearby observations, but should reduce to 0 correlation for observations further apart.

# First for bartmod0, one panel for each chain
bartmod0 = step1.res$bartmod0

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod0$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 1],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod0$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 2],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod0$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 3],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod0$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 4],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

# Then for bartmod1
bartmod1 = step1.res$bartmod1

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod1$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 1],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod1$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 2],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod1$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 3],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

auto.corr <- acf(bartmod1$yhat.train[ , sample(1:dim(dat)[1], 10), 4],
                 plot = FALSE)
max.lag <- max(auto.corr$lag[ , 1, 1])

j <- seq(-0.5, 0.4, length.out = 10)
for (h in 1:10) {
  if (h == 1) {
    plot(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
         type = 'h', xlim = c(0, max.lag + 1), ylim = c(-1, 1),
         ylab = 'acf', xlab = 'lag')
  } else {
    lines(1:max.lag + j[h], auto.corr$acf[1 + (1:max.lag), h, h],
          type = 'h', col = h)
  }
}

Next, we plot the Geweke Z statistics for each individual, which should be approximately distributed as a standard Normal.

# First for bartmod0
geweke <- gewekediag(bartmod0$yhat.train.collapse)
n <- dim(dat)[1]
j <- -10^(log10(n) - 1)
plot(geweke$z, pch = '.', cex = 2, ylab = 'z', xlab = 'i',
     xlim=c(j, n), ylim=c(-5, 5))
lines(1:n, rep(-1.96, n), type='l', col=6)
lines(1:n, rep(+1.96, n), type='l', col=6)
lines(1:n, rep(-2.576, n), type='l', col=5)
lines(1:n, rep(+2.576, n), type='l', col=5)
lines(1:n, rep(-3.291, n), type='l', col=4)
lines(1:n, rep(+3.291, n), type='l', col=4)
lines(1:n, rep(-3.891, n), type='l', col=3)
lines(1:n, rep(+3.891, n), type='l', col=3)
lines(1:n, rep(-4.417, n), type='l', col=2)
lines(1:n, rep(+4.417, n), type='l', col=2)
text(c(1, 1), c(-1.96, 1.96), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.95')
text(c(1, 1), c(-2.576, 2.576), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.99')
text(c(1, 1), c(-3.291, 3.291), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.999')
text(c(1, 1), c(-3.891, 3.891), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.9999')
text(c(1, 1), c(-4.417, 4.417), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.99999')

# Then for bartmod1
geweke <- gewekediag(bartmod1$yhat.train.collapse)
plot(geweke$z, pch = '.', cex = 2, ylab = 'z', xlab = 'i',
     xlim=c(j, n), ylim=c(-5, 5))
lines(1:n, rep(-1.96, n), type='l', col=6)
lines(1:n, rep(+1.96, n), type='l', col=6)
lines(1:n, rep(-2.576, n), type='l', col=5)
lines(1:n, rep(+2.576, n), type='l', col=5)
lines(1:n, rep(-3.291, n), type='l', col=4)
lines(1:n, rep(+3.291, n), type='l', col=4)
lines(1:n, rep(-3.891, n), type='l', col=3)
lines(1:n, rep(+3.891, n), type='l', col=3)
lines(1:n, rep(-4.417, n), type='l', col=2)
lines(1:n, rep(+4.417, n), type='l', col=2)
text(c(1, 1), c(-1.96, 1.96), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.95')
text(c(1, 1), c(-2.576, 2.576), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.99')
text(c(1, 1), c(-3.291, 3.291), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.999')
text(c(1, 1), c(-3.891, 3.891), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.9999')
text(c(1, 1), c(-4.417, 4.417), pos=2, cex=0.6, labels='0.99999')

If several points lie beyond the dark blue line or further, consider using more thinning when fitting the models.
